title: Python Overview
subtitle: Parent topic showing Python and data engineering study branches

nodes.label: Python
nodes.definition: A general-purpose programming language widely used for automation, data engineering, analytics, and application development.
nodes.whyItMatters: Python is the base language that connects many data engineering tools and workflows.
nodes.safeSentence: Python is the foundation language that lets me automate, transform data, and connect systems.

note.summary: Python is the parent skill. Pandas and PySpark are deeper study branches from it.

nodes.label: Pandas
nodes.definition: A Python library for tabular data analysis, cleaning, grouping, joining, and transformation.
nodes.whyItMatters: Pandas is often the first practical tool for shaping data before scaling patterns to larger systems.
nodes.safeSentence: Pandas is excellent for local tabular data work and for prototyping data transformations.

note.summary: Pandas is a child topic because it deserves its own map.

nodes.label: PySpark
nodes.definition: The Python API for Apache Spark, used for distributed data processing across larger datasets and clusters.
nodes.whyItMatters: PySpark extends Python-style data engineering into distributed processing.
nodes.safeSentence: PySpark is where the data engineering pattern scales beyond local memory.

note.summary: PySpark can become its own child topic later.

paths.label: Python to Data Tools
paths.description: Python is the base language, Pandas supports local tabular work, and PySpark extends the pattern to distributed data.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how Python connects Pandas and PySpark in data engineering.
